definitions: An instrument or device used for making loops.
The caterpillar of a geometer moth, which moves by drawing the hind part of the body up to the forepart.
An electronic device that creates instant recordings of a performance and plays them back in real-time loops.
A person who performs looping or ADR in film post-production.
examples: The guitarist used a looper to layer his tracks.
A green looper crawled across the leaf.
The tailor adjusted the looper on the sewing machine.
